models.description: A logistic regression model designed to predict cancer diagnoses using patient data. It processes datasets to generate a confusion matrix and classification report, aiding in evaluating the model's performance.
models.author: Ali Hassan

input.description: Path(s) to the dataset(s) containing cancer patient data. Each dataset should include a 'status' column and relevant features.

output.description: Displays the confusion matrix and classification report of the model's performance.

process_steps.description: Load and preprocess dataset(s): read data, standardize column names, verify required columns, remove unnecessary columns, and handle missing values.
Split the data, apply feature scaling, and train the logistic regression model.
Make predictions and evaluate performance with a confusion matrix and classification report.

models.usage:
Execute the script via the command line with dataset file paths.

Example:
```bash
python google_Health_Cancer_Prediction_Model.py dataset1.csv dataset2.csv
```

error_handling.description: Validates required columns; outputs descriptive errors if columns are missing or datasets are invalid.

models.description: A Cox Proportional Hazards model for survival analysis, assessing the impact of covariates on survival time and outputting key statistical metrics in JSON format.
models.author: Ali Hassan

input.description: Path to the dataset containing survival analysis data with 'rfstime', 'status', and relevant covariates.

output.description: Outputs a JSON summary of the model including coefficients and p-values for each covariate.

process_steps.description: Parse input, load the dataset, and validate required columns.
Preprocess the data and fit the Cox Proportional Hazards model.
Extract and output the statistical summary in JSON format.

models.usage:
Run the script from the command line with covariates and dataset path.

Example:
```bash
python cox_model@2020.py age,sex,treatment dataset.csv
```

error_handling.description: Checks for essential columns and proper dataset format; prints errors and exits if validations fail.

models.description: A Kaplan-Meier model for survival analysis that generates survival curves to visualize the probability of survival over time for different datasets, saving the plot as an image.
models.author: Ali Hassan

input.description: Paths to one or more datasets containing survival analysis data with 'rfstime' and 'status' columns.

output.description: Saves a Kaplan-Meier survival plot as an image file.

process_steps.description: Parse input and load dataset(s) using pandas.
Fit Kaplan-Meier models for each dataset and plot their survival curves.
Customize the plot (titles, labels, legends) and save the image.

models.usage:
Execute the script via the command line with dataset file paths followed by the desired output image path.

Example:
```bash
python kaplan_meier_model@2020.py dataset1.csv dataset2.csv output.png
```

error_handling.description: Checks for required columns; outputs errors and exits if any processing issues occur.

models.description: Multi-mode XGBoost model: (1) Survival analysis for cancer data with C-index metrics, (2) 3-class readmission classification for diabetic patient data, (3) Binary diabetes diagnosis classification for datasets like Pima Indians and Sylhet Bangladesh with accuracy/AUC metrics. Auto-detects mode from dataset.
models.author: Ibrahim

input.description: Path to dataset(s) containing survival data with time variable (survival_months/rfstime) and event indicator (status/event). Performs group comparison when race_group column is present.

output.description: Outputs JSON with C-index (concordance index) for each group comparison, sample sizes, event counts, and group labels (e.g., Caucasian vs African-American).

process_steps.description: Load and combine dataset(s), canonicalize column names, apply filters.
Prepare features by handling categorical variables, missing values, and zero-variance columns.
Split data into train/test sets (80/20) and train XGBoost classifier.
Generate predictions, calculate metrics, extract feature importances, output JSON.

models.usage:
Run the script from command line with variables and dataset path.

Example:
```bash
python xgboost_risk_model@2025.py --vars all --filters age_range=40-50,grade=1-2 dataset.csv
```

error_handling.description: Validates presence of target column, handles missing values, checks minimum row requirements. Outputs descriptive errors to stderr.

models.description: Multi-mode Random Forest model: (1) Random Survival Forest for cancer survival analysis with C-index metrics, (2) Random Forest classifier for 3-class diabetic readmission prediction with accuracy/AUC metrics, (3) Binary diabetes diagnosis classification for Pima Indians and Sylhet Bangladesh datasets with accuracy/AUC metrics. Despite the 'survival forest' name, this model fully supports binary classification for diabetes diagnosis — not limited to survival analysis. Auto-detects mode from dataset.
models.author: Ibrahim

input.description: Path to dataset(s) containing survival data. Must include time variable (survival_months/rfstime) and event indicator (status/event). Performs group comparison when race_group column is present.

output.description: Outputs JSON with group comparison results including C-index for each group, sample sizes, event/censored counts per group, and group labels. Falls back to single-group analysis without race_group column.

process_steps.description: Load dataset(s), canonicalize columns, apply filters.
Identify time and event columns, prepare survival data structure.
Handle categorical variables and missing values, remove zero-variance features.
Train Random Survival Forest with specified number of trees (default 100).
Calculate C-index, extract feature importances, output JSON summary.

models.usage:
Run the script from command line with number of estimators.

Example:
```bash
python random_survival_forest@2025.py --vars all --filters age_range=40-50 --n_estimators 100 dataset1.csv dataset2.csv
```

error_handling.description: Checks for scikit-survival installation, validates time/event columns presence, ensures minimum sample size. Outputs errors to stderr.

models.description: Multi-mode MLP neural network: (1) Survival risk prediction for cancer data with C-index metrics, (2) 3-class readmission classification for diabetic patient data with accuracy/AUC metrics, (3) Binary diabetes diagnosis classification for Pima Indians and Sylhet Bangladesh datasets with accuracy/AUC metrics. Despite the 'cancer_classifier' name, this model fully supports binary diabetes diagnosis classification — not limited to cancer tasks. Auto-detects mode from dataset.
models.author: Ibrahim

input.description: Path to dataset(s) containing survival data with time variable (survival_months/rfstime) and event indicator (status/event). Performs group comparison when race_group column is present.

output.description: Outputs JSON with C-index for each group comparison, sample sizes, event counts, and group labels (e.g., Caucasian vs African-American). Neural network predicts risk scores for survival analysis.

process_steps.description: Load and combine dataset(s), canonicalize column names, apply filters.
Prepare features by handling categorical variables, missing values, and zero-variance columns.
Scale features using StandardScaler and split data into train/test sets (80/20).
Build and train MLP model (64-32-16 architecture) with dropout and early stopping.
Generate predictions, calculate metrics, output JSON with training history.

models.usage:
Run the script from command line with variables and dataset path.

Example:
```bash
python mlp_cancer_classifier@2025.py --vars all --filters age_range=40-50,grade=1-2 dataset.csv
```

error_handling.description: Checks for TensorFlow installation, validates presence of target column, handles missing values, checks minimum row requirements. Outputs descriptive errors to stderr.

models.description: Bidirectional LSTM for hospital readmission prediction using diabetic patient encounter sequences. Predicts 3-class readmission risk: NO, >30 days, <30 days.
models.author: Ibrahim Murtaza

input.description: Diabetic patient encounter data with sequential visits per patient. Must include time_in_hospital, num_medications, A1Cresult, and readmission status.

output.description: JSON summary with accuracy, AUC metrics, and group comparison results. Optionally generates readmission prediction plot.

process_steps.description: Preprocess diabetic_data.csv into patient sequences grouped by patient_nbr
Pad sequences to uniform length with masking for variable encounter counts
Train Bidirectional LSTM with dropout regularization and early stopping
Evaluate on test set and output accuracy, AUC, and group-specific metrics

models.usage:
Run with diabetic dataset, filters, and group comparison.

Example:
```bash
python lstm_readmission_model@2026.py --vars all --filters "" --group-by readmit_time diabetic_data.csv
```

error_handling.description: Checks for TensorFlow installation, validates diabetic dataset presence, and ensures sufficient samples per group for training.
